gold_change_reason: Added handling for missing 'Charge' column (default '0'), missing 'ProteinName' column (skip mapping), deduplication in protein mapping, and tolerance for extra columns via header_dict lookup.
covered_requirements: Reinforce the constraint that the protein mapping must not contain duplicate peptide names for the same protein.
Add adversarial robustness by testing that the function works correctly when the 'ProteinName' column is missing.
Add an optional 'Modifications' column to the CSV header and row, ensuring the function uses header_dict and ignores extraneous columns.
Handle a variant where the row contains an extra column not referenced in the problem description.
Handle a variant where mapRow is called multiple times to check deduplication.
Add adversarial robustness by testing that the function works correctly when the 'Charge' column is completely missing.
Handle a variant where the 'Charge' column is missing.
Handle a variant where the 'ProteinName' column is missing.
implementation_notes: The function uses header_dict to locate columns, so extra columns are automatically ignored.
When 'Charge' column is missing or has 'NA'/'', charge defaults to '0'.
When 'ProteinName' column is missing, protein mapping is not updated.
Deduplication in protein mapping: peptide names are only added if not already present.
Sequences mapping accumulates all keys from each call (allows duplicates) to match expected behavior.
Empty trailing entries from split(';') are removed from transition lists.
